name: Snow Drifts
recipeIngredient: 2 tbsp Nonfat egg substitute, equivalent to 1/2 egg
6 Filo dough sheets, thawed according to package directions
recipeInstructions:
1. The flaky texture of filo dough combined with a creamy lemon curd filling makes a great-tasting, low-fat dessert.
2. Instead of using butter, spray the sheets of filo dough with butter-flavored, non-stick cooking spray to keep the fat content low.
3. The lemon curd contains almost no fat. Only a small amount of nonfat egg substitute and one teaspoon of butter were added to the filling for flavor.
4. In a medium saucepan, combine granulated sugar, cornstarch, and salt. Stir in boiling water until blended.
5. Heat, stirring constantly, to boiling. Continue heating and stirring until thickened and clear, about 1 minute.
6. Add some of the hot cornstarch mixture to the egg substitute, stirring until blended.
7. Return all ingredients to the saucepan. Heat and stir over low heat until slightly thickened, being careful not to curdle the mixture.
8. Stir in butter until melted. Stir in lemon zest and juice. Let cool to room temperature, then chill.
9. Place one sheet of filo on a cutting board. Spray evenly with butter-flavored cooking spray. Repeat layering with second and third sheets of filo dough.
10. Cut stacked sheets into eight strips lengthwise. Cut strips in half crosswise to make 16.
11. Arrange one strip of filo sheets into each of the 16 mini-muffin cups to form a nest shape. Repeat with remaining three sheets of filo dough.
12. Bake at 350°F for 5-7 minutes or until golden brown. Remove to a wire rack to cool.
13. Dust filo cups with powdered sugar. Fill with lemon curd. Makes 32 pastries. Each pastry contains about: 34 calories; 35 milligrams sodium; trace cholesterol; trace fat; 8 grams carbohydrates; 1 gram protein; 0 fiber.
